rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant The Board Of Trustees Of The Leland Stanford Junior University¿S Filing Of A Memorandum Of Costs As Untimely
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion to strike defendant The Board of Trustees of the Leland Stanford Junior University¿s filing of a memorandum of costs as untimely is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL PRATT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Timec Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE ALLEN WALL, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 8, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Mallinckrodt Baker, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of the negligence and products liability causes of action is denied. Mr. Wall¿s deposition testimony, Mr. Benning¿s deposition testimony and declaration, Ms. Matlosz¿s deposition testimony, Ms. Shields¿ declaration submitted by plaintiffs, the VRW catalog and Dr. Dahlgren¿s declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Wall was exposed to raw asbestos or asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
